# Measure Claude sibling provider-capacity independence

## Context

Parent Epic #11812 graduated Discussion #11792 (*Instance-addressable wake routing for parallel Claude identities*) into a measurement-first path for a possible second Claude-family maintainer identity.

The parent epic deliberately gates Desktop-grade instance-addressable wake-routing implementation behind OQ8: a second Claude identity is only useful if it has independent usable Anthropic capacity or materially improves usable turn cadence under Neo's real lifecycle workload.

Live pre-creation checks:

- #11812 is open and carries AC1-AC4 requiring provider-capacity measurement before Desktop-routing implementation begins.
- #11792 is now closed RESOLVED with `[GRADUATED_TO_TICKET: #11812]`, `gpt` AUTHOR_SIGNAL, and `claude` GRADUATION_APPROVED.
- Duplicate sweep over open GitHub issues for `provider-capacity`, `Fast Mode`, `Claude sibling`, and `OQ8` found #11812 and unrelated adjacent items, but no bounded measurement-only sub-ticket.
- `rg` over `resources/content/issues` / `resources/content/discussions` found other OQ8 uses, but no Claude sibling provider-capacity measurement ticket.

## The Problem

The throughput premise behind a Claude sibling is currently unproven. Running two Claude identities can improve swarm throughput only if they do not merely share the same slow provider queue, quota bottleneck, or Fast Mode behavior.

Without a bounded measurement sub-ticket, future implementation work can accidentally spend substrate budget on Desktop wake-routing mechanics before proving the cheaper upstream premise.

## The Architectural Reality

Relevant parent constraints from #11812:

- The sibling remains `modelFamily: claude`; it can increase throughput and same-family pressure, but it does not satisfy cross-family PR approval for Claude-authored work.
- Terminal/tmux may be used only as a measurement/control instrument. A passing terminal measurement does not graduate terminal/tmux as the product harness.
- Desktop-grade cadence is a second-stage measurement after provider-capacity independence passes.
- Fast Mode is a separate variable. Operator spot-check says disabling Fast Mode did not feel slower, but that is qualitative evidence, not a measured conclusion.

This sub does not implement `AgentIdentity`, `WakeSubscriptionService`, `bridge-daemon`, or memory-continuity changes. It produces the measurement evidence that decides whether those later subs should proceed.

## The Fix

Run a controlled measurement of Claude sibling provider-capacity independence:

1. Define a repeatable Neo workload representative of high-context / high-effort lifecycle pressure: skill loading, issue/PR context, A2A coordination, and public-artifact drafting.
2. Measure a baseline single Claude identity under comparable conditions.
3. Measure a second Claude account/seat or sibling identity candidate under the cheapest valid harness available, with terminal/tmux allowed only as OQ8 instrument/control.
4. Hold Fast Mode constant for the first pass, then run a small Fast Mode enabled vs disabled A/B if the harness supports it.
5. Record wall-clock turn latency, time-to-first-useful-output, completion quality/review corrections, operator subjective usability, quota/rate-limit symptoms, and burst-capacity economics.
6. Produce one of three dispositions: `PASS_OQ8`, `FAIL_OQ8`, or `INCONCLUSIVE_WITH_BLOCKER`.

## Contract Ledger Matrix

| Target Surface | Source of Authority | Proposed Behavior | Fallback | Docs | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Provider-capacity measurement | #11812 AC1-AC4 and Discussion #11792 OQ8 | Produce a comparable measurement showing whether a second Claude account/seat has independent usable capacity | If no account/seat is available, record `INCONCLUSIVE_WITH_BLOCKER` and do not proceed to Desktop-routing implementation | Measurement note on this issue or linked artifact | L1/L2 timing log + operator usability observations |
| Fast Mode variable | #11812 AC2 and Discussion #11792 OQ6 | Treat Fast Mode as a separate A/B variable, not as an assumed improvement | If mode control is unavailable, record as limitation | Measurement note | A/B table or explicit limitation |
| Terminal/tmux use | #11812 terminal false-completion constraint | May be used only as OQ8 instrument/control; not product completion | Desktop-grade cadence remains separate after OQ8 passes | Measurement note | Explicit non-goal declaration |
| Disposition gate | #11812 AC4 | `FAIL_OQ8` parks/closes Desktop-routing implementation path before substrate work starts | `INCONCLUSIVE_WITH_BLOCKER` keeps parent blocked without implementation | #11812 comment and child issue closeout | Disposition comment with measured evidence |

## Decision Record Impact

none. This is a measurement gate under Epic #11812. It does not change accepted ADRs or Agent OS substrate. A later implementation PR may need ADR or Agent OS decision-record review if it introduces a durable wake-routing primitive.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] AC1 — Measurement workload is defined and includes at least one high-context/high-effort Neo lifecycle task shape.
- [ ] AC2 — Baseline single-Claude timing and usability data are recorded.
- [ ] AC3 — Candidate sibling account/seat timing and usability data are recorded, or unavailable-account blocker is documented.
- [ ] AC4 — Fast Mode is held constant for the first comparison and either A/B-tested separately or explicitly marked unavailable.
- [ ] AC5 — Measurement records wall-clock turn latency, time-to-first-useful-output, completion quality/review corrections, operator subjective usability, and quota/rate-limit symptoms.
- [ ] AC6 — Terminal/tmux, if used, is explicitly marked measurement-only and not product completion.
- [ ] AC7 — Final disposition is one of `PASS_OQ8`, `FAIL_OQ8`, or `INCONCLUSIVE_WITH_BLOCKER`.
- [ ] AC8 — #11812 is updated/commented with the disposition before any Desktop-routing implementation sub begins.

## Out of Scope

- Implementing Desktop-grade instance-addressable wake routing.
- Creating the Claude sibling AgentIdentity.
- Changing PR review governance or cross-family approval arithmetic.
- Treating terminal/tmux as the maintainer product harness.
- Procuring credentials or exposing Anthropic account secrets in any artifact.

## Avoided Traps

| Trap | Why rejected |
|---|---|
| Build before measuring | #11812 explicitly gates implementation behind this premise. |
| Terminal false completion | A terminal/tmux pass can prove capacity independence only; it does not satisfy Desktop-grade workflow. |
| Fast Mode confounding | Fast Mode must be controlled or isolated so the sibling measurement does not mix two variables. |
| API capacity equals harness capacity | The measurement is about usable Neo workflow cadence, not marketing/model-card context. |

## Board routing update — external measurement gate

Live watchdog intake verified #11816 is not a GPT-claimable implementation lane today.

Evidence:
- #11816 is the upstream OQ8 measurement gate for #11812.
- The issue requires external Claude account/seat or sibling-candidate measurement evidence, or an explicit `INCONCLUSIVE_WITH_BLOCKER` disposition if no account/seat is available.
- Downstream children #11821 and #11822 are already natively `blockedBy` #11816 and have been removed from Project 12 Todo.
- No implementation branch should start from #11816 without the external measurement input; this remains an operator/Claude evidence lane, not release-board ready work.

Action taken:
- Removed the legacy `agent-task:blocked` label.
- Removed #11816 from Project 12 Todo so the release board does not present it as claimable ready work.
- Left the issue open as the measurement anchor for #11812.

Unblock condition: record `PASS_OQ8`, `FAIL_OQ8`, or `INCONCLUSIVE_WITH_BLOCKER` with measurement evidence / account-availability evidence, then route #11812 accordingly.
